title: Shared library reuse without privacy leakage
preconditions: Local curriculum content library is empty or has a known baseline.
Cloud-library remote access remains disabled for the default pilot posture.
Two learners can request the same curriculum-shaped content key.
setup_path: Run the seeded household setup through scripts/rehearse_proof_scenarios.py.
Use Avery and Blair from the proof fixture as two learners in the same household.
Keep DIBBLE_CLOUD_LIBRARY_ENABLED=false for the default local-only privacy rehearsal.
execution_path: POST /api/content/generate for learner A with a curriculum-shaped KC/theme/modality request.
POST /api/content/generate for learner B with the same curriculum-shaped request.
Confirm learner B receives a cache/library hit and the audit has no forbidden field hits.
visible_outcome: Learner B reuses learner A's verified curriculum artifact while the privacy audit reports only sentinel student ids and curriculum-shaped request context.
approval_hooks: Cloud-library remote read and publish remain governed by rollout policy; the default proof path is local_only.

rehearsal_steps.action: Generate verified content for learner A against a specific KC, modality, theme, locale, and scaffolding level.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: The generated artifact is stored with a curriculum content key and provenance.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: Verified content becomes reusable without storing learner identity in the library contract.

rehearsal_steps.action: Have learner B request the same curriculum-shaped content key.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: The library path can serve or rank the existing artifact before fresh generation.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: The library compounds across learners while staying curriculum-shaped.

rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: Trace shows curriculum metadata, provenance, verification state, and local/remote fallback status.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: Reuse is explainable and operationally visible.

rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: No learner_id, student_id, household_id, session_id, response_text, or profile fields appear in the content key or payload.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: The milestone-1 privacy boundary remains intact.

expected_observations: Library entries are keyed by curriculum metadata rather than learner identity.
Remote disabled state falls back to local-only behavior without blocking learning.
Operational traces make remote lookup or publish degradation visible when remote access is enabled later.
success_criteria: Learner B can reuse or rank content generated during learner A's flow.
The library artifact contains only curriculum-shaped metadata and content.
Remote-library degradation is visible and falls back to local-only behavior.
reset_notes: Clear curriculum_content_library rows or use a new KC/theme/scaffolding combination to repeat the miss-then-hit pattern.
